conditions: The Dart Trap is a trap that can be found naturally placed in Cavern layer and Dungeon . It fires a dart when triggered via wire which does not consume any ammunition . It has a cooldown of (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) 3 1/3 seconds / (Old-gen console and 3DS versions) 3 seconds between firing. The darts deal 40 / 80 / 120 damage [ 1 ] and inflict Poisoned for 10 / 20 / 25 seconds to players (20 damage and 10 seconds of Poisoned to NPCs , enemies , and critters ). [ 2 ]

Daybloom is a type of herb that appears as a small yellow flower with a straight green stem. It grows naturally on regular grass and Hallowed grass . Daybloom can be harvested with virtually any weapon or tool , and can be planted manually using Daybloom Seeds in Clay Pots and Planter Boxes , or on suitable soil tiles .

Daybloom Seeds are a type of seed that can be obtained from harvesting Dayblooms while they are blooming or using the Staff of Regrowth on naturally grown Dayblooms while they are fully grown. Daybloom will only bloom during daytime , showing off a sunflower-like head. Each plant will drop 1–3 Daybloom Seeds when harvested while blooming.

notes: The Eternia Crystal is an event-summoning item. When used on the Eternia Crystal Stand while under the correct terrain requirements , the Old One's Army event begins. The Eternia Crystal can be purchased from the Tavernkeep for 25 SC (pre-one mechanical boss ), 1 GC (post-one mechanical boss), or 4 GC (post- Golem ).

The Eternia Crystal Stand is a furniture item that initiates the Old One's Army event when an Eternia Crystal is placed on it with ⚷ Open / Activate . The Eternia Crystal Stand can be purchased from the Tavernkeep for 1 GC .

The Killing Deck is a Hardmode magic weapon that can be purchased from the Wizard for 20 GC during a Blood Moon . When used, three waves of cards are launched. The first launch consists of a single card, the second of two cards launched in opposite directions, and the third of a spread of six cards. These cards fly for 0.5 seconds in a serpentine pattern before abruptly falling. They stick to any block or enemy they hit, but stop dealing damage after becoming stuck. The cards return to the player upon using the weapon for the fourth time, or after being stuck to a block or enemy for more than 5 seconds. While returning, the cards deal 150% of the weapon's damage (33 base damage) and pierce infinitely. [ 1 ]
The Swarm Grenade is a pet-summoning item that spawns a Swarm Biter that follows the player. The Swarm Grenade can be purchased from the Zoologist for 10 GC during a Party .
Normal mode row

chanceText: 2.5% (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) 0.4% (Old-gen console and 3DS versions) 4.94%
notes: Normal mode row

conditions: Deathweed is a type of herb which appears as a short, withered purple stalk. It grows naturally on Ebonstone , Crimstone , Corrupt grass , Crimson grass , Corrupt Jungle grass , and Crimson Jungle grass . Deathweed can be harvested with virtually any weapon or tool , and can be planted manually using Deathweed Seeds in Clay Pots and Planter Boxes , or on suitable soil tiles .

Deathweed Seeds are a type of seed that can be obtained from harvesting Deathweeds while they are blooming or using the Staff of Regrowth on naturally grown Deathweeds while they are fully grown. A Deathweed plant only blooms during Blood Moons and Full Moons . Its blooming state can be identified by the sickly red pods on its leaves. Each plant will drop 1–3 Deathweed Seeds when harvested while blooming.
In addition to being crafted, Deer Things also have a 1/5 (20%) chance of being found in Frozen Chests found in the Ice biome (but not Snow).

notes: The Defender's Forge is a portable, personal storage item which is purchased from the Tavernkeep for 500000* 50 .

quantityText: Demon Heart (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) (when no Demon Heart used yet)

notes: The Demon Horns are a vanity item purchased from the Traveling Merchant for 2 GC 50 SC .
Normal mode row

conditions: Demonite Ore is a pre-Hardmode ore . It is primarily dropped from the Eater of Worlds , as well as the Eye of Cthulhu in Corruption worlds. Demonite Ore can also be found rarely in small veins underground . When below 0 ft in elevation, a Gold Pickaxe or better is needed to mine it, otherwise any pickaxe can be used.

Demonite Ore is a pre-Hardmode ore . It is primarily dropped from the Eater of Worlds , as well as the Eye of Cthulhu in Corruption worlds. Demonite Ore can also be found rarely in small veins underground . When below 0 ft in elevation, a Gold Pickaxe or better is needed to mine it, otherwise any pickaxe can be used.

The Sandstone Chest is a storage item found in the Underground Desert . They are usually found in Underground Cabins but may also uncommonly generate by themselves. Sandstone Chests can also be crafted.

Each naturally-generated Sandstone Chest will always contain one primary item, and may contain one or two secondary items. Besides one of the items listed here, they will also contain common items depending on the height they were found in: Underground or Caverns .

Desert Fossil is a type of block found in the Underground Desert biome in medium to large veins. Desert Fossils can be used with an Extractinator or Chlorophyte Extractinator to produce Sturdy Fossils , along with regular Extractinator drops.

Detonators are activation Mechanisms that can be found underground as part of Traps , wired to Explosives . They are often found planted on top of suspiciously large veins of underground metals (E.g. Silver ).

notes: The Devil Horns are a vanity item that can be purchased from the Traveling Merchant for 2 GC 50 SC .

conditions: Diamond is a gem found within the Underground and Cavern layers of a world. They are obtained by breaking special blocks that have noticeable light blue gemstones embedded in them. They can also be extracted from Silt , Slush , or Desert Fossil by putting them in an Extractinator . Rarely, Diamonds can be found in its own Gemstone Cave mini biome, or in one that contains all of the other gems as well (besides Amber ).

notes: The Diamond Ring is a vanity accessory . When worn, it places a blue dot on each of the player's wrists. It is purchased from the Traveling Merchant for 2 PC .

conditions: The Dirt Block is one of the most common blocks in Terraria , occurring naturally almost everywhere throughout the world. All caves above the elevation of 0 ft are made of Dirt. Below 0 ft, in the underground layer, the caves are made of roughly 50% Dirt. Small patches of Dirt can be found in the cavern layer, though it is mostly made of Stone .

notes: The Dirt Rod is purchased from the Dryad for (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) 5 GC / (Old-gen console and 3DS versions) 20 GC .

conditions: The Dirt Wall is a type of background wall which can be found naturally generated in most caves and under overhangs on the surface , considered unsafe . It does not drop as an item when destroyed, and can only be obtained via crafting. It is considered a safe wall when placed by the player.

notes: The Disco Ball is a novelty Hardmode light source that can be placed on the underside of blocks , generating short pulses of colored light that cycle through the spectrum (In the Nintendo 3DS version , the Disco Ball will cycle through red, green, and blue only). The light can be toggled with wires . It is purchased from the Merchant in Hardmode for 1 GC each.

notes: The Guide to Critter Companionship is a pre-Hardmode tool that prevents the player's attacks from harming critters while inside their inventory or opened Void Bag . It can be purchased from the Zoologist for 5 GC .
The Guide to Environmental Preservation is a pre-Hardmode tool that prevents the player's attacks from breaking plants while inside their inventory . It can be purchased from the Dryad for 5 GC .

conditions: Double Cod are a type of fish which are found uncommonly by fishing in the Sky or Surface layers of the Jungle . They are used to craft Seafood Dinners and Ammo Reservation Potions .

notes: The DPS Meter can be purchased from the Traveling Merchant for 5 GC .

The Drum Set is a novelty furniture item used in conjunction with the Drumstick to produce drum sounds. The Drum Set and Drumstick can be purchased from the Merchant for 10 GC and 50 SC respectively after either the Eater of Worlds , the Brain of Cthulhu , Skeletron , or the Wall of Flesh has been defeated.
The Dye Vat is a crafting station used to craft dyes , paints , and colored string and Golf Balls . It is purchased from the Dye Trader for 5 GC .
Dynamite can be found in Chests or purchased from the Demolitionist for 20 SC each.
Dynasty Wood is a type of wood purchased from the Traveling Merchant for 50 CC per block. When placed, it forms continuous structures with automatic frames.
Easter Blocks are pre-Hardmode blocks purchased from the Dye Trader for 50 CC each. Their main use is crafting Easter furniture and Easter Walls .

conditions: Ebonkoi are a type of fish which are found uncommonly by fishing in the Corruption in any layer. They are used to craft Seafood Dinners and Wrath Potions .

Ebonstone is a block type found in The Corruption . Before Hardmode , it is most prominent in the lining of Chasms . It requires at least a Nightmare Pickaxe or explosives to mine (this can be bypassed by using Purification Powder to turn Ebonstone to normal Stone , which is mineable with any pickaxe). Deathweed can grow on it.

The Ebonstone Wall is a type of background wall which can be found naturally generated in The Corruption , considered unsafe . It does not drop as an item when destroyed, and can only be obtained on Desktop , Console , and Mobile , via crafting. It is considered a safe wall when placed by the player.

Ebonwood is a type of wood harvested from Ebonwood trees and Ebonwood Palm trees found naturally growing in Corruption biomes. Ebonwood trees can be cultivated by placing Acorns on top of Corrupt grass or Corrupt Jungle grass above surface , and Ebonwood Palm trees can be cultivated by placing Acorns on top of Ebonsand Blocks above surface.

Ebonwood is a type of wood harvested from Ebonwood trees and Ebonwood Palm trees found naturally growing in Corruption biomes. Ebonwood trees can be cultivated by placing Acorns on top of Corrupt grass or Corrupt Jungle grass above surface , and Ebonwood Palm trees can be cultivated by placing Acorns on top of Ebonsand Blocks above surface.

notes: Normal mode row

conditions: Emerald is a gem found within the Underground and Cavern layers of a world. They are obtained by breaking special blocks that have noticeable green gemstones embedded in it. They can also be extracted from Silt , Slush , or Desert Fossil by putting them in an Extractinator . Rarely, Emeralds can be found in bulk in its own Gemstone Cave mini biome, or in one that contains all of the other gems as well (besides Amber ).

quantityText: Terraprisma (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) (if 100% of damage is dealt at daytime)

conditions: The Prismatic Lacewing is a rare Hardmode , post- Plantera flying critter and boss-summoning item that spawns on the surface Hallow after Plantera has been defeated. Prismatic Lacewings disappear almost instantaneously when spawned in any other biome, and only spawn in the Hallow between dusk (7:30 PM) and midnight (12:00 AM). When killed by a player, it summons the Empress of Light ; death from non-player sources will generally not result in summoning. Only one Prismatic Lacewing can be naturally spawned at a time.

notes: Empty Bullets are a Hardmode crafting material purchased from the Arms Dealer for 5 CC (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) / 3 CC (Old-gen console and 3DS versions) each. While they have no function as ammunition on their own, Empty Bullets are used to craft Hardmode bullets with special effects.

The Empty Dropper is a Hardmode crafting material purchased from the Wizard for 1 SC . It is used in the crafting of Magic Droppers . The Crystal Ball must be near water , lava , or honey , to craft the respective liquid's dropper.

quantityText: Enchanted Pixie Dust (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) (Only after one mechanical boss has been defeated)

conditions: The Enchanted Sword can be obtained by destroying an "enchanted sword in stone" background object, which can be found in random locations underground and in Enchanted Sword Shrines . Destroying this background object has a 29/30 (96.67%) chance to drop an Enchanted Sword, with the other 1/30 (3.33%) times instead dropping a Terragrim . The Enchanted Sword also has a 1/30 (3.33%) and 1/15 (6.67%) chance to be found in Golden and Titanium Crates respectively.
The Encumbering Stone has a 1/7 (14.29%) chance of being found in Sandstone Chests in the Underground Desert .

notes: The Engineering Helmet is a vanity item equipped on the Social Helmet Slot . It is purchased from the Mechanic for 1 GC .

The Exotic Chew Toy is a pet-summoning item that spawns a Fennec Fox that follows the player. When the player is far enough away or in midair, the Fennec Fox pet will fly by flapping its ears like wings to catch up to them. The Exotic Chew Toy can be purchased from the Traveling Merchant for 1 PC .

Explosive Powder is a Hardmode crafting material purchased from the Demolitionist for 12 SC each, once the Wall of Flesh has been defeated. It is solely used to make Exploding Bullets , an ammo type with strong knockback .

conditions: Explosives can spawn naturally as traps wired to Pressure Plates , or wired to Detonators (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) next to a vein of ores can be found in the Underground and Cavern layer.

notes: The Extendo Grip is a construction accessory that is purchased from the Traveling Merchant for 10 GC . While equipped, it increases placement range and tool range by 2 tiles vertically and 3 tiles horizontally.
Normal mode row

conditions: The Fairy Bell is a Hardmode light pet-summoning item that spawns a Fairy , a glowing creature resembling a Pixie , which follows the player and produces light for an unlimited duration. The summoned Fairy will be either pink, green, or blue, the color of which changes randomly each time the Fairy is summoned. The Fairy is faster and brighter than the Shadow Orb and Crimson Heart , but not as fast or bright as the Wisp in a Bottle .
